questions: I feel energized and resilient today. It's Monday morning and you have a full week of meetings, deadlines, and commitments ahead. How do you feel looking at your calendar?
I feel energized and resilient today. A friend asks you to help them move this weekend, which would be your third weekend in a row without a break. What do you say?
I feel energized and resilient today. Your boss adds another project to your plate on top of the three you're already managing. How do you respond?
I feel energized and resilient today. You get home after a long day and your partner wants to have a serious conversation about finances. How do you approach it?
I feel energized and resilient today. Your child has had a meltdown before school for the fourth morning in a row and you need to handle it before leaving for work. How do you feel?
I feel energized and resilient today. You've been dealing with a family member's ongoing health issue for months and they call with another update that requires action. What goes through you?
I feel energized and resilient today. A coworker quits unexpectedly and their workload gets distributed to the remaining team, including you. How do you take the news?

I feel energized and resilient today. You're running errands after work and realize you still need to go to the grocery store, pick up dry cleaning, and return a package. How do you feel about the list?
I feel energized and resilient today. An old problem you thought was resolved resurfaces and needs your attention again. How do you react?
I feel energized and resilient today. You sit down at the end of a long day and realize you still have emails to answer and tomorrow looks just as packed. What's your state of mind?
